13580	[The base-changed cover intersection is the preimage of the intersection]
13581	  \label{lem:coverinteropen_basechange_eq}
13582	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverInterOpen_baseChange_eq}
13583	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve}
13584	  Let \(g' : X' \to X\) be the pullback of a base change \(g : S' \to S\) along a separated
13585	  \(f : X \to S\), and let \(\mathfrak{U}'\) be the base change along \(g'\) of the affine
13586	  open-immersion cover \(\mathfrak{U}\) of \(X\). Then for every finite index family
13587	  \(\sigma\) the {\v C}ech intersection open of the base-changed cover is the preimage of the
13588	  intersection open of the original cover:
13589	  \[
13590	    \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathfrak{U}'\,\sigma
13591	      \;=\; (g')^{-1}\bigl(\operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathfrak{U}\,\sigma\bigr).
13592	  \]
13593	\end{lemma}
13594	\begin{proof}
13595	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve}
13596	  The base-changed cover member \(\mathfrak{U}'.X\,i\) is by definition the pullback
13597	  \((g')^{-1}(U_i)\), so its range in \(X'\) is the preimage of the range of \(U_i\). Taking
13598	  preimages of opens commutes with finite meets (the inverse-image map on the frame of opens
13599	  preserves intersections), whence
13600	  \(\operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathfrak{U}'\,\sigma = \bigcap_k (g')^{-1}(U_{\sigma(k)})
13601	  = (g')^{-1}\bigl(\bigcap_k U_{\sigma(k)}\bigr)
13602	  = (g')^{-1}\bigl(\operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathfrak{U}\,\sigma\bigr)\).
13603	\end{proof}
13604	
13605	\begin{lemma}
13606	
13607	\leanok
13608	[Open-immersion Beck--Chevalley over a restricted cartesian square]
13609	  \label{lem:openimm_beckchevalley}
13610	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_beckChevalley}
13611	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj}
13612	  Let
13613	  \[
13614	    \begin{array}{ccc}
13615	      V' & \xrightarrow{\ g'_V\ } & V\\
13616	      {\scriptstyle p'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle p}\\
13617	      X' & \xrightarrow{\ g'\ } & X
13618	    \end{array}
13619	  \]
13620	  be the cartesian square obtained by restricting \(g' : X' \to X\) over an \emph{open
13621	  immersion} \(p : V \hookrightarrow X\) \emph{with \(V\) affine}, so that
13622	  \(p' : V' \hookrightarrow X'\) is again an open immersion (the pullback of \(p\) along
13623	  \(g'\), with image the preimage \((g')^{-1}(V)\)). Assume in addition that \(X\) is
13624	  \emph{separated} (so that the affine \(V\) meets every affine open of \(X\) in an affine;
13625	  in Lean this is a separatedness hypothesis on \(X\) --- e.g.\ \([\mathrm{IsSeparated}\,
13626	  (\mathrm{terminal.from}\,X)]\), or any equivalent input guaranteeing affine
13627	  intersections, at the prover's choice). Then for any \emph{quasi-coherent}
13628	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) (\(\mathtt{hF} : \mathcal{F}.\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\))
13629	  there is a Beck--Chevalley isomorphism
13630	  \[
13631	    (g')^*\bigl(p_*\,p^*\mathcal{F}\bigr)
13632	      \;\cong\;
13633	    p'_*\,p'^*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr),
13634	  \]
13635	  i.e.\ \((g')^*(\operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,(\operatorname{Over.mk} p)) \cong
13636	  \operatorname{pushPullObj}((g')^*\mathcal{F})\,(\operatorname{Over.mk} p')\). This is the
13637	  open-immersion (in particular flat) case of Beck--Chevalley. The affineness of \(V\)
13638	  together with the quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{F}\) is what reduces it, after refining
13639	  \(X'\) by an affine open cover, to the sorry-free affine termwise base-change brick
13640	  \cref{lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange}. The separatedness hypothesis on \(X\) is
13641	  what makes the affine \(V\) meet each affine open of \(X\) in an affine; in the application
13642	  the consumer discharges it from its own separatedness hypothesis through
13643	  \cref{lem:cech_interopen_isaffine}.
13644	\end{lemma}
13645	\begin{proof}
13646	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, lem:openimm_bareBC, lem:openimm_bc_telescope,
13647	        lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib, lem:openimm_bareBC_isIso}
13648	  Throughout write \(G := p^*\mathcal{F}\) for the restricted module on \(V\). A warning on the
13649	  geometry first: \(p\) being an open immersion does \emph{not} make the \emph{right}-adjoint
13650	  direct image \(p_*\) extension-by-zero. Extension-by-zero is the \emph{left} adjoint \(p_!\);
13651	  the right adjoint \(p_*\) has genuinely nonzero sections off \(V\) (over an open \(U \subseteq
13652	  X\) its sections are \(\Gamma(U \cap V, p^*\mathcal{F})\), not \(0\) for \(U \not\subseteq V\)).
13653	  So there is no ``both sides vanish away from \(V'\)'' support argument. The off-\(V'\) data is
13654	  real and is exactly the content of the comparison; the correct tool is a sectionwise criterion.
13655	  The orientation is the open-immersion (flat) special case of base change, Stacks Project
13656	  Tag~02KH (\texttt{lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology}); the section-level input for Stage~2 is
13657	  supplied, after refining \(X'\) by an affine open cover and using that \(V\) is affine and
13658	  \(\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent, by the affine termwise base-change brick
13659	  \cref{lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange} (Stacks Project Tag~02KG), which already
13660	  packages the affine tilde dictionaries and the cancellation
13661	  \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\).
13662	
13663	  \emph{Stage 1 (telescope; no affineness required).} Since \(p\) and \(p'\) are open immersions,
13664	  pullback along them \emph{is} restriction: the restriction functor and the module pullback
13665	  agree, \(\operatorname{restrict} p \cong p^*\) and \(\operatorname{restrict} p' \cong p'^*\)
13666	  (\cref{lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}). Using the pseudofunctor structure of pullback
13667	  --- the comparison \((q \circ r)^* \cong r^* \circ q^*\) for composable morphisms, together with
13668	  reindexing of \((-)^*\) along an equality of morphisms --- and the commuting square
13669	  \(g'_V \circ p' = p \circ g'\) (in the sense \(p' \cdot g' = g'_V \cdot p\) as maps
13670	  \(V' \to X\)), one telescopes
13671	  \[
13672	    p'^*\,(g')^*
13673	      \;\cong\; (p' \cdot g')^*
13674	      \;=\; (g'_V \cdot p)^*
13675	      \;\cong\; (g'_V)^*\,p^* .
13676	  \]
13677	  Applied to \(\mathcal{F}\) this identifies \(p'^*(g')^*\mathcal{F} \cong (g'_V)^* G\), hence the
13678	  right-hand side rewrites as
13679	  \(p'_*\,p'^*(g')^*\mathcal{F} \cong p'_*\,(g'_V)^* G = (\operatorname{pushforward} p')
13680	  ((g'_V)^* G)\), while the left-hand side is
13681	  \((g')^*\bigl(p_*\,G\bigr) = (g')^*\bigl((\operatorname{pushforward} p) G\bigr)\). The whole
13682	  telescope is formal and uses no affineness. It reduces the lemma, with the single fixed
13683	  coefficient \(G = p^*\mathcal{F}\), to the assertion that the \emph{bare base-change map}
13684	  \[
13685	    \operatorname{bareBC} \colon\quad
13686	    (g')^*\bigl((\operatorname{pushforward} p)\,G\bigr)
13687	      \;\longrightarrow\;
13688	    (\operatorname{pushforward} p')\bigl((g'_V)^* G\bigr)
13689	  \]
13690	  --- the canonical mate of \(\operatorname{pushforward} p \circ (g')^* \to (g'_V)^* \circ
13691	  \operatorname{pushforward} p'\) attached to the cartesian square --- is an isomorphism. (This
13692	  canonical mate is the local comparison morphism; it is stated from first principles,
13693	  independently of any relative-spectrum base-change theory.)
13694	
13695	  \emph{Stage 2 (the bare comparison is an isomorphism).} It remains only to prove that
13696	  \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) is an isomorphism. This is exactly \cref{lem:openimm_bareBC_isIso}.
13697	  By the mate formula, \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) at \(G\) is the \(p'\)-unit at
13698	  \(M = (g')^*(p_*\,G)\) followed by isomorphisms (the telescope components and the \(p\)-counit,
13699	  invertible for the open immersion \(p\) by \cref{lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso}); since
13700	  \(p'_*\) is fully faithful, the unit is an isomorphism iff \(M\) lies in the essential image of
13701	  \(p'_*\), and that membership is cover-local (\cref{lem:essimage_pushforward_cover_local}).
13702	  Check it on an affine open cover \(\{W_j\}\) of \(X'\) refining
13703	  \((g')^{-1}(\text{affine opens of } X)\) (\cref{lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover}): over each
13704	  member \(W_j = \operatorname{Spec} B\), with \(g'(W_j) \subseteq U_j = \operatorname{Spec} A\),
13705	  --- because \(V\) is affine and \(X\) is separated --- the intersection \(V \cap U_j\) is
13706	  affine, the restricted local square is a \emph{fully-affine} pullback, and the sorry-free
13707	  affine termwise base-change isomorphism \cref{lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange},
13708	  applied with the open immersion \(V \cap U_j \hookrightarrow U_j\) in the role of the affine
13709	  map and presenting module \(M' = \Gamma(V \cap U_j,\ p^*\mathcal{F})\) (quasi-coherent by
13710	  \(\mathtt{hF}\)), exhibits \(M|_{W_j}\) as a pushforward from \(W_j \cap (g')^{-1}(V)\)
13711	  (\cref{lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine}). Hence \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) is an
13712	  isomorphism. Combined with the Stage~1 reduction this proves the lemma.
13713	
13714	  \emph{Remark (the hypotheses are essential).} The affineness of \(V\), the separatedness of
13715	  \(X\), and the quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{F}\) are not cosmetic. Separatedness is what makes
13716	  the affine \(V\) meet each affine open \(U_j\) of \(X\) in an \emph{affine} intersection
13717	  \(V \cap U_j\); without it the local square over \(W_j\) need not be fully affine and the
13718	  reduction to the affine brick breaks. The outer base pullback \((g')^*\) is the sheafification
13719	  of the presheaf tensor \(U \mapsto (p_*\,p^*\mathcal{F})(U) \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}
13720	  \mathcal{O}_{X'}(W)\); this tensor description agrees with the sections of
13721	  \(p'_*\,(g'_V)^*(p^*\mathcal{F})\) only on the \emph{quasi-coherent} locus, so for a general
13722	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module the comparison fails --- quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{F}\) is genuinely
13723	  required. Affineness of \(V\) is what makes the local square over each \(W_j\) fully affine and
13724	  hence reducible to the affine brick; in the application it is supplied by the consumer, where
13725	  each fibre power \(U_\sigma\) of the {\v C}ech nerve is affine because the cover is affine and
13726	  the structure morphism is separated (\cref{lem:cech_interopen_isaffine}). In particular the
13727	  bare-mate route never forms the global push--pull \(p_*\,p^*\mathcal{F}\), so no
13728	  quasi-compactness of \(p\) is needed; there is no claim of a hypothesis-free open-immersion
13729	  Beck--Chevalley.
13730	\end{proof}
13731	
13732	% NOTE (status, 2026-07-02 session 0003-horizon-T2): the soundness re-signing flagged by the
13733	% earlier note is DONE (openImmersion_beckChevalley carries `[IsAffine V]`,
13734	% `hF : F.IsQuasicoherent`, `[IsSeparated (terminal.from X)]`).  Stage 2 is now decomposed
13735	% differently (and more finely) than the original legs-restriction plan: the mate formula
13736	% factors bareBC.app as (p'-unit) ≫ isos (\cref{lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso} inverts the
13737	% p-counit), fully-faithful p'_* turns the unit node into essential-image membership, membership
13738	% is cover-local (\cref{lem:essimage_pushforward_cover_local}, sorry-free), and the SOLE residual
13739	% is the affine-local member node \cref{lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine}
13740	% (`openImmersion_pushPull_essImage_member`).
13741	
13742	% =====================================================================================
13743	% Stage-2 decomposition chain for lem:openimm_beckchevalley (carved iter-329, slug obc329).
13744	% These are an INTERNAL decomposition of the already-cited open-immersion Beck--Chevalley
13745	% proof (Stacks Tags 02KH/02KG/01I9, quoted at lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso /
13746	% lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange in Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex and at the
13747	% target lemma above); no new external source is introduced. Lean homes: namespace
13748	% AlgebraicGeometry in Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean (next to
13749	% openImmersion_bareBC).
13750	% =====================================================================================
13751	
13752	\begin{lemma}\leanok
13753	[Affine cover of \(X'\) refining preimages of affine opens of \(X\)]
13754	  \label{lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover}
13755	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_refiningAffineCover}
13756	  \uses{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib}
13757	  Let \(g' : X' \to X\) be any morphism of schemes. Then there is an affine open cover
13758	  \(\{W_j\}\) of \(X'\) together with, for each \(j\), an affine open \(U_j \subseteq X\) such that
13759	  \(g'(W_j) \subseteq U_j\); equivalently the restricted map \(g'|_{W_j} : W_j \to U_j\) is a map
13760	  of affine schemes, hence a \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-map \(g'|_{W_j} = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi_j\)
13761	  for a ring map \(\varphi_j : \Gamma(U_j, \mathcal{O}_X) \to \Gamma(W_j, \mathcal{O}_{X'})\).
13762	\end{lemma}
13763	\begin{proof}
13764	  \uses{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib}
13765	  Take the standard affine open cover \(\mathfrak{A} = \{A_i = \operatorname{Spec} R_i\}\) of \(X\)
13766	  and pull it back along \(g'\): the base-changed cover \(g'^{-1}\mathfrak{A}\) is an open cover of
13767	  \(X'\) whose member over index \(i\) maps into the affine \(A_i\). Its members \(g'^{-1}(A_i)\)
13768	  need not be affine, so refine each of them by its own affine open cover and combine the results
13769	  into a single cover of \(X'\). The resulting cover \(\{W_j\}\) is affine, and each \(W_j\) sits
13770	  inside some \(g'^{-1}(A_i)\), whence \(g'(W_j) \subseteq A_i =: U_j\). A morphism between two
13771	  affine schemes is induced by a ring map on global sections (\cref{lem:isoSpec_scheme_mathlib}),
13772	  so \(g'|_{W_j} = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi_j\) with \(\varphi_j = \Gamma(g'|_{W_j})\).
13773	\end{proof}
13774	
13775	\begin{lemma}\leanok
13776	[Stage 2, reduction (1): the geometric counit at an open immersion is invertible]
13777	  \label{lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso}
13778	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_pullback_counit_isIso}
13779	  \uses{lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
13780	  Let \(q : V \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion of schemes.  Then for every
13781	  \(\mathcal{O}_V\)-module \(c\) the counit \(q^*(q_*\,c) \to c\) of the pullback--pushforward
13782	  adjunction \(q^* \dashv q_*\) is an isomorphism.  (Equivalently: \(q_*\) is fully faithful for
13783	  an open immersion \(q\).)
13784	\end{lemma}
13785	\begin{proof}\leanok
13786	  \uses{lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
13787	  For an open immersion the module pullback \(q^*\) is naturally isomorphic to the restriction
13788	  functor (\cref{lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}), and the counit of the
13789	  restriction--pushforward adjunction is invertible: over an open \(O \subseteq V\) it is the
13790	  identification \(\Gamma(O,\ \operatorname{restrict}_q (q_*\,c)) = \Gamma(q^{-1}(q(O)),\ c) =
13791	  \Gamma(O,\ c)\), using that \(q\) is an open embedding so \(q^{-1}(q(O)) = O\).  Counits of
13792	  adjunctions with a common right adjoint correspond under the canonical comparison of left
13793	  adjoints, so the geometric counit is the composite of an isomorphism (the comparison, evaluated
13794	  at \(q_*\,c\)) with an isomorphism (the restriction counit), hence an isomorphism.
13795	\end{proof}
13796	
13797	\begin{lemma}\leanok
13798	[Stage 2, reduction (2): restriction maps of a pushforward-presented module are isomorphisms]
13799	  \label{lem:restrictionMap_isIso_of_essImage}
13800	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.restrictionMap_isIso_of_essImage}
13801	  Let \(W\) be a scheme, \(O_0 \subseteq W\) an open with inclusion \(\iota : O_0 \hookrightarrow
13802	  W\), and \(N\) an \(\mathcal{O}_W\)-module lying in the essential image of \(\iota_*\), say
13803	  \(N \cong \iota_*\,K\).  Then for every open \(O \subseteq W\) the presheaf restriction map
13804	  \[
13805	    N(O) \longrightarrow N(O \cap O_0)
13806	  \]
13807	  is an isomorphism.
13808	\end{lemma}
13809	\begin{proof}\leanok
13810	  Transport along the isomorphism \(N \cong \iota_*\,K\) (naturality of its components in the
13811	  open).  On the pushforward side the map in question is the restriction
13812	  \((\iota_*K)(O) = K(\iota^{-1}(O)) \to K(\iota^{-1}(O \cap O_0)) = (\iota_*K)(O \cap O_0)\),
13813	  and \(\iota^{-1}(O \cap O_0) = \iota^{-1}(O) \cap \iota^{-1}(O_0) = \iota^{-1}(O)\) since
13814	  \(\iota^{-1}(O_0)\) is everything; so it is \(K\) applied to an identity inclusion, hence an
13815	  isomorphism.
13816	\end{proof}
13817	
13818	\begin{lemma}\leanok
13819	[Stage 2, reduction (3): essential-image membership for \(p'_*\) is cover-local]
13820	  \label{lem:essimage_pushforward_cover_local}
13821	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.essImage_pushforward_of_openCover,
13822	        AlgebraicGeometry.restrictFunctor_map_app}
13823	  \uses{lem:isIso_iff_isIso_restrict, lem:restrictionMap_isIso_of_essImage,
13824	        lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
13825	  Let \(p' : V' \hookrightarrow X'\) be an open immersion with open range \(R = p'(V')\), let
13826	  \(M\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_{X'}\)-module, and let \(\{W_j\}\) be an open cover of \(X'\).  If
13827	  for every \(j\) the restriction \(M|_{W_j}\) lies in the essential image of the pushforward
13828	  along the open inclusion \(W_j \cap R \hookrightarrow W_j\), then \(M\) lies in the essential
13829	  image of \(p'_*\).
13830	\end{lemma}
13831	\begin{proof}\leanok
13832	  \uses{lem:isIso_iff_isIso_restrict, lem:restrictionMap_isIso_of_essImage,
13833	        lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
13834	  Since \(p'_*\) is fully faithful (\cref{lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso}), \(M\) lies in its
13835	  essential image iff the unit \(M \to p'_*\,p'^*\,M\) of \(p'^* \dashv p'_*\) is an isomorphism.
13836	  Under the identification of \(p'^*\) with restriction
13837	  (\cref{lem:restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}) the unit has, over an open \(U \subseteq X'\),
13838	  the plain presheaf restriction component \(M(U) \to M(U \cap R)\).  Invertibility of this
13839	  morphism of sheaves is local on \(X'\) (\cref{lem:isIso_iff_isIso_restrict}): it suffices that
13840	  each restriction to \(W_j\) be invertible, and — because restriction along an open immersion
13841	  has definitional components — the restricted morphism has, over an open \(O \subseteq W_j\),
13842	  again the plain component \(M(w_j(O)) \to M(p'(p'^{-1}(w_j(O))))\).  By the lattice identity
13843	  \(w_j(O \cap w_j^{-1}(R)) = w_j(O) \cap R = p'(p'^{-1}(w_j(O)))\) this component is, up to the
13844	  induced identification of the target sections, the restriction map
13845	  \(M|_{W_j}(O) \to M|_{W_j}(O \cap w_j^{-1}(R))\), which is an isomorphism by
13846	  \cref{lem:restrictionMap_isIso_of_essImage} applied to the member datum.  Hence the unit is an
13847	  isomorphism and \(M\) lies in the essential image of \(p'_*\).
13848	\end{proof}
13849	
13850	\begin{lemma}
13851	[Stage 2, member node: over each affine \(W_j\) the pulled-back push--pull module is a pushforward]
13852	  \label{lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine}
13853	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_pushPull_essImage_member}
13854	  \uses{lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange,
13855	        lem:pullback_preserves_quasicoherent, lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso,
13856	        lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover}
13857	  Keep the data of \cref{lem:openimm_beckchevalley}, and assume explicitly the hypotheses this
13858	  member node consumes: \(V\) affine (\([\mathrm{IsAffine}\,V]\)), \(X\) separated (the
13859	  separatedness predicate of \cref{lem:openimm_beckchevalley} --- e.g.\ \([\mathrm{IsSeparated}\,
13860	  (\mathrm{terminal.from}\,X)]\), or an equivalent affine-intersection input, at the prover's
13861	  choice), and \(\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent (\(\mathtt{hF} : \mathcal{F}.\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\)).
13862	  Write \(M := (g')^*(p_*\,p^*\mathcal{F})\).  Let \(W_j = \operatorname{Spec} B \subseteq X'\),
13863	  \(U_j = \operatorname{Spec} A \subseteq X\) be as in \cref{lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover},
13864	  with \(g'|_{W_j} = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi\), \(\varphi : A \to B\). Since \(V\) is affine and
13865	  \(X\) is separated, the intersection \(V \cap U_j\) is affine, say \(V \cap U_j =
13866	  \operatorname{Spec} A'\), and the open immersion \(V \cap U_j \hookrightarrow U_j\) is
13867	  \(\operatorname{Spec}\) of a ring map \(A \to A'\). Then the restriction \(M|_{W_j}\) lies in
13868	  the essential image of the pushforward along the open inclusion
13869	  \(W_j \cap (g')^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow W_j\).
13870	\end{lemma}
13871	\begin{proof}
13872	  \uses{lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange,
13873	        lem:pullback_preserves_quasicoherent, lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso,
13874	        lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover}
13875	  By the pullback pseudofunctor along \(w_j \cdot g' = g'|_{W_j} \cdot (U_j \hookrightarrow X)\),
13876	  the restriction \(M|_{W_j}\) is the pullback along \(g'|_{W_j} = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) of
13877	  the restriction \((p_*\,p^*\mathcal{F})|_{U_j}\); the open-restriction identity
13878	  \((p_*\,p^*\mathcal{F})|_{U_j} = (V \cap U_j \hookrightarrow U_j)_*\,(p^*\mathcal{F}|_{V \cap U_j})\)
13879	  (both sides are site-level pushforwards along the same opens functor) presents that restriction
13880	  as \(f_*\widetilde{M'}\) for \(f = \operatorname{Spec}(A \to A')\) and the
13881	  \(A'\)-module \(M' = \Gamma(V \cap U_j,\ p^*\mathcal{F})\). This \(M'\) exists because
13882	  \(p^*\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent (the restriction \(p^*\) of the quasi-coherent
13883	  \(\mathcal{F}\), \cref{lem:pullback_preserves_quasicoherent}) and a quasi-coherent module over an
13884	  affine is the \(\widetilde{(-)}\) of its sections (\cref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}).
13885	  Applying \(\operatorname{Spec}\) to the ring pushout
13886	  \[
13887	    \begin{array}{ccc}
13888	      A & \xrightarrow{\ \varphi\ } & B\\
13889	      \downarrow & & \downarrow\\
13890	      A' & \longrightarrow & B \otimes_A A'
13891	    \end{array}
13892	  \]
13893	  (cut out by the affine map \(\varphi : A \to B\) presenting \(g'|_{W_j}\) and the affine open
13894	  immersion \(A \to A'\) presenting \(V \cap U_j \hookrightarrow U_j\)) gives a fully-affine
13895	  cartesian square, and the affine termwise base-change isomorphism
13896	  \[
13897	    g^*\bigl(f_*\widetilde{M'}\bigr) \;\cong\; f'_*\bigl((g')^*\widetilde{M'}\bigr)
13898	  \]
13899	  of \cref{lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange} for this pushout square (with
13900	  \(g = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi\)) exhibits \(M|_{W_j}\) as the pushforward of
13901	  \((g')^*\widetilde{M'}\) along \(f' = \operatorname{Spec}(B \to B \otimes_A A')\), the base
13902	  change of the open immersion \(V \cap U_j \hookrightarrow U_j\); its range is
13903	  \(g'|_{W_j}^{-1}(V \cap U_j) = W_j \cap (g')^{-1}(V)\).  Transporting the pushforward along
13904	  this range identification, \(M|_{W_j}\) lies in the essential image of the pushforward along
13905	  \(W_j \cap (g')^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow W_j\), as claimed.
13906	\end{proof}
13907	
13908	\begin{lemma}
13909	[Stage 2, assembly: \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) is an isomorphism]
13910	  \label{lem:openimm_bareBC_isIso}
13911	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_bareBC_app_eq,
13912	        AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_bareBC_app_isIso_of_unit,
13913	        AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_unit_isIso_of_essImage,
13914	        AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_pushPull_essImage,
13915	        AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_pushPull_unit_isIso}
13916	  \uses{lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso, lem:essimage_pushforward_cover_local,
13917	        lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover, lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine,
13918	        lem:openimm_bareBC, lem:openimm_bc_telescope,
13919	        lem:isOpenImmersion_of_isPullback_left}
13920	  For the cartesian square \(p' \cdot g' = g'_V \cdot p\) of \cref{lem:openimm_beckchevalley} with
13921	  \(p\) an open immersion, \(V\) affine (\([\mathrm{IsAffine}\,V]\)), \(X\) separated, and
13922	  \(\mathcal{F}\) a \emph{quasi-coherent} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
13923	  (\(\mathtt{hF} : \mathcal{F}.\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\)), the bare base-change map
13924	  \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) (\cref{lem:openimm_bareBC}), evaluated at \(G = p^*\mathcal{F}\), is an
13925	  isomorphism. (These hypotheses are essential: the proof applies the affine member node
13926	  \cref{lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine}, which needs each \(V \cap U_j\) affine ---
13927	  hence \(V\) affine and \(X\) separated --- and needs \(p^*\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent ---
13928	  hence \(\mathtt{hF}\). The arbitrary-\(\mathcal{F}\) form is false.)
13929	\end{lemma}
13930	\begin{proof}
13931	  \uses{lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso, lem:essimage_pushforward_cover_local,
13932	        lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover, lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine,
13933	        lem:openimm_bareBC, lem:openimm_bc_telescope,
13934	        lem:isOpenImmersion_of_isPullback_left}
13935	  Write \(M := (g')^*(p_*\,G)\).  \emph{Mate factorization.}  By the componentwise mate formula,
13936	  \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) evaluated at \(G\) is the composite of the unit
13937	  \(M \to p'_*\,p'^*\,M\) of \(p'^* \dashv p'_*\), followed by \(p'_*\) applied to the pullback
13938	  telescope components (\cref{lem:openimm_bc_telescope}, isomorphisms), followed by
13939	  \(p'_*\,(g'_V)^*\) applied to the counit \(p^*(p_*\,G) \to G\) --- an isomorphism because
13940	  \(p\) is an open immersion (\cref{lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso}).  Hence
13941	  \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) is an isomorphism as soon as the leading unit is.
13942	
13943	  \emph{The unit is an isomorphism.}  Since \(p'\) is an open immersion
13944	  (\cref{lem:isOpenImmersion_of_isPullback_left}), \(p'_*\) is fully faithful
13945	  (\cref{lem:openimm_pullback_counit_isIso}), so the unit at \(M\) is an isomorphism iff \(M\)
13946	  lies in the essential image of \(p'_*\).  That membership is cover-local
13947	  (\cref{lem:essimage_pushforward_cover_local}); check it on the affine open cover \(\{W_j\}\) of
13948	  \(X'\) refining \((g')^{-1}(\text{affine opens of } X)\) supplied by
13949	  \cref{lem:openimm_refining_affine_cover}: over each member the restriction \(M|_{W_j}\) is a
13950	  pushforward from \(W_j \cap (g')^{-1}(V)\) by the member node
13951	  \cref{lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine}.  Hence \(M\) is in the essential image, the unit is
13952	  an isomorphism, and \(\operatorname{bareBC}\) is an isomorphism.
13953	\end{proof}
13954	
13955	\begin{lemma}
13956	[Open immersions are stable under base change]
13957	  \label{lem:isOpenImmersion_stableUnderBaseChange_mathlib}
13958	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isOpenImmersion_stableUnderBaseChange}
13959	  \mathlibok
13960	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
13961	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.OpenImmersion}).}
13962	  The class of open immersions of schemes is stable under base change: in any pullback square,
13963	  the base change of an open immersion is again an open immersion.
13964	\end{lemma}
13965	
13966	\begin{lemma}
13967	[Left base change of an open immersion is an open immersion]
13968	  \label{lem:isOpenImmersion_of_isPullback_left}
13969	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isOpenImmersion_of_isPullback_left}
13970	  \uses{lem:isOpenImmersion_stableUnderBaseChange_mathlib}
13971	  Given a cartesian square with maps \(g_V, p', p, g'\) satisfying \(p' \cdot g_V = g'
13972	  \cdot p\), if \(p\) is an open immersion then so is its base change \(p'\).
13973	
13974	  This supplies the open-immersion property of \(p'\) needed for the target-side restriction in
13975	  Stage~2 of \cref{lem:openimm_beckchevalley}.
13976	\end{lemma}
13977	\begin{proof}
13978	  \uses{lem:isOpenImmersion_stableUnderBaseChange_mathlib}
13979	  Open immersions are stable under base change
13980	  (\cref{lem:isOpenImmersion_stableUnderBaseChange_mathlib}); applying that stability to the given
13981	  cartesian square turns the open immersion \(p\) into the open immersion \(p'\).
13982	\end{proof}
13983	
13984	\begin{lemma}\leanok
13985	[Bare Beck--Chevalley mate of the restricted square]
13986	  \label{lem:openimm_bareBC}
13987	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_bareBC}
13988	  \uses{lem:openimm_bc_telescope}
13989	  For the cartesian square \(p' \cdot g' = g'_V \cdot p\) of \cref{lem:openimm_beckchevalley} there
13990	  is a canonical natural transformation --- the \emph{bare base-change map} ---
13991	  \[
13992	    \operatorname{bareBC} \colon\quad
13993	    \operatorname{pushforward} p \circ (g')^*
13994	      \;\Longrightarrow\;
13995	    (g'_V)^* \circ \operatorname{pushforward} p',
13996	  \]
13997	  namely the Beck--Chevalley \emph{mate}, under the two pullback--pushforward adjunctions
13998	  \(p^* \dashv p_*\) and \(p'^* \dashv p'_*\), of the canonical pullback \(2\)-isomorphism
13999	  \((g')^* \circ p'^* \cong p^* \circ (g'_V)^*\) attached to the square. It exists for \emph{any}
14000	  morphisms forming the cartesian square --- no flatness, no open-immersion hypothesis --- and its
14001	  being an isomorphism is the genuine Beck--Chevalley content discharged in Stage~2 of
14002	  \cref{lem:openimm_beckchevalley}.
14003	\end{lemma}
14004	\begin{proof}
14005	  Assemble the pullback \(2\)-isomorphism
14006	  \((g')^* p'^* \cong (p' \cdot g')^* = (g'_V \cdot p)^* \cong (g'_V)^* p^*\) by combining
14007	  the pseudofunctor composition isomorphism for \(p' \cdot g'\), the reindexing along the square
14008	  equation \(p' \cdot g' = g'_V \cdot p\), and the inverse composition isomorphism for
14009	  \(g'_V \cdot p\) (the content of \cref{lem:openimm_bc_telescope}). The mate construction for the adjunctions \(p^* \dashv p_*\) and
14010	  \(p'^* \dashv p'_*\) turns this \(2\)-isomorphism into the stated natural transformation. The
14011	  construction is formal and uses no hypothesis on the maps.
14012	\end{proof}
14013	
14014	\begin{lemma}\leanok
14015	[Pullback telescope across the restricted square]
14016	  \label{lem:openimm_bc_telescope}
14017	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.openImmersion_bc_telescope}
14018	  In the cartesian square of \cref{lem:openimm_beckchevalley}, the iterated pullback of any
14019	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) telescopes:
14020	  \[
14021	    p'^*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr)
14022	      \;\cong\; (p' \cdot g')^*\mathcal{F}
14023	      \;=\; (g'_V \cdot p)^*\mathcal{F}
14024	      \;\cong\; (g'_V)^*\bigl(p^*\mathcal{F}\bigr),
14025	  \]
14026	  using only the pseudofunctor comparison \((q \cdot r)^* \cong r^* \circ q^*\) and reindexing of
14027	  \((-)^*\) along the square equation \(p' \cdot g' = g'_V \cdot p\). No flatness and no
14028	  affineness hypotheses are needed.
14029	\end{lemma}
14030	\begin{proof}
14031	  Compose the pseudofunctor composition isomorphism for \(p' \cdot g'\), the reindexing
14032	  isomorphism along \(p' \cdot g' = g'_V \cdot p\), and the inverse composition isomorphism for
14033	  \(g'_V \cdot p\), then evaluate the resulting natural isomorphism at \(\mathcal{F}\).
14034	\end{proof}
14035	
14036	\begin{lemma}
14037	
14038	\leanok
14039	[Per-intersection-open X-level Beck--Chevalley for the twisted nerve]
14040	  \label{lem:twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma}
14041	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma}
14042	  \uses{lem:coverinteropen_basechange_eq, lem:openimm_beckchevalley, lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde}
14043	  In the setting of \cref{lem:twisted_cech_nerve_iso}, fix an index family \(\sigma\) with
14044	  intersection open \(U_\sigma = \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathfrak{U}\,\sigma\),
14045	  \(j_\sigma : U_\sigma \hookrightarrow X\), and write \(U'_\sigma\),
14046	  \(j'_\sigma : U'_\sigma \hookrightarrow X'\) for the corresponding data of the base-changed
14047	  cover \(\mathfrak{U}'\). Then there is the per-intersection-open \(X\)-level Beck--Chevalley
14048	  isomorphism
14049	  \[
